title: centaurea × noguerensis
intro: Centaurea × noguerensis, also known as the hybrid cornflower, is a plant species resulting from a cross between Centaurea montana and Centaurea corymbosa. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a demulcent and anti-inflammatory agent, due to its high content of mucilages and saponins. The health benefits of Centaurea × noguerensis include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and alleviate respiratory problems. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for consumption, or used in topical applications to benefit from its medicinal properties.
